REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ANGELA OLIVERI, FIRST WESTERN PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INDEMNITY IS MOOT; DISMISSAL ON FILE. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THE EXISTENCE OF A WRITTEN CONTRACT OR ESTABLISHING EXCEPTION TO THE STATUE OF FRAUDS. PLAINTIFF SHALL ALSO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. THE COURT WILL NOT FOLLOW AFONT AT THIS TIME, BUT MAY DO SO WHEN THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IS PROPERLY ASSERTED IF, AS SUCH, IT APPEARS COMPLETELY DUPLICATIVE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE. PLAINTIFF SHALL FURTHER ALLEGE MISREPRESENTATION WITH PARTICULARITY (WHEN, HOW, BY WHAT MEANS, WHERE, ETC), ESPECIALLY AS AGAINST A CORPORATE DEFENDANT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SCOTT LAEBER, COLLIERS INTERNATIONAL CA, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INDEMNITY IS MOOT; DISMISSAL ON FILE. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO THE SPECIFIC DUTY TO INVESTIGATE ZONING. REFERENCES MADE IN OPPOSITION TO GENERAL STATUTES ESTABLISHING OBLIGATIONS OF THE BROKERS AND GENERAL REFERENCES TO THE LETTER OF INTENT/LEASE ARE NOT SUFFICIENT. THE ISSUE HERE IS NOT THE EXISTENCE OF A CLIENT/BROKER RELATIONSHIP, THE ISSUE IS THE SPECIFIC DUTY TO INVESTIGATE ZONING - THE SOURCE OF THIS DUTY IS NOT CURRENTLY ALLEGED. PLAINTIFF SHALL FURTHER ALLEGE MISREPRESENTATION WITH PARTICULARITY.PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED AND JOINT STIPULATION TO CONTINUE HEARINGSON DEMURRERS TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100131011) FILED BY DEFENDANT LAEBER, SCOTT COLLIERS INTERNATIONAL CA, INC.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SCOTT LAEBER, COLLIERS INTERNATIONAL CA, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, ESTABLISHING EACH ELEMENT OF THESE CAUSE OF ACTION, INCLUDING "WHO, WHAT, WHEN, WHERE, AND HOW." DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONCEALMENT IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, SHOWING THAT THIS DEFENDANT KNEW ABOUT ZONING ISSUES. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE A CONTRACT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND THESE DEFENDANTS, THE CONTRACTUAL CLAUSE BREACHED AND DAMAGES. LETTER OF INTENT IS NOT A CONTRACT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND THIS DEFENDANT. TO THE EXTENT CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY ARE BASED ON THE FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE, DEMURRER TO THESE CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IF PLAINTIFF IS ABLE RE-ALLEGE THESE CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON FACTS/THEORY OTHER THAN FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE, PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND TO SO ALLEGE. ALL AMENDMENTS TO BE IN BOLD FONT. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; COURT REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO, CSR # 5229, PEGGYTSUJIMOTO@GMAIL.COM, 415-828-1919 (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jill Rowe; Venable LLP; 415-653-3750; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Colliers International CA, Inc. and Scott Laeber.
Scott J. Loeding; Murchison & Cumming, LLP; 213-623-7400; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants First Western Properties, Inc. and Angela Oliveri.
James G. Higgins; Dunn & Panagotacos LLP; 415-982-2100; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiff Mobileone, LLC.

Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR # 5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SCOTT LAEBER, COLLIERS INTERNATIONAL CA, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained. Demurrer to the causes of action for intentional and negligent misrepresentation is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, establishing each element of these cause of action, including "who, what, when, where, and how." Demurrer to the cause of action for concealment is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, showing that this defendant knew about zoning issues. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of contract is sustained with final leave to amend to allege a contract between Plaintiff and these defendants, the contractual clause breached and damages. Letter of Intent is not a contract between Plaintiff and this defendant. To the extent causes of action for professional negligence and breach of fiduciary duty are based on the failure to investigate, demurrer to these causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. If plaintiff is able re-allege these causes of action based on facts/theory other than failure to investigate, Plaintiff shall have final leave to amend to so allege. All amendments to be in bold font. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR # 5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919 (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jill Rowe; Venable LLP; 415-653-3750; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Colliers International CA, Inc. and Scott Laeber.
Scott J. Loeding; Murchison & Cumming, LLP; 213-623-7400; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants First Western Properties, Inc. and Angela Oliveri.
James G. Higgins; Dunn & Panagotacos LLP; 415-982-2100; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiff Mobileone, LLC.

Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR # 5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ANGELA OLIVERI, FIRST WESTERN PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts taking this matter outside the statute of frauds; allege facts supporting a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty as applicable under the circumstances of this case; and to allege misrepresentation by these defendants with requisite particularity. All amendments to be in bold font. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR # 5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919 (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery By Defendants Colliers International Ca, Inc. And Scott Laeber; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Filed Concurrently)
Off Calendar Jan-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MOBILEONE LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery By Defendants Colliers International Ca, Inc. And Scott Laeber; Request For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted except for RFPD No. 7 and RFA Nos. 4,5,7,8 for which the motion is denied as the requests are either vague or overbroad. The Court notes that while Judge Quidachay did sustain Defendants' demurrer, leave to amend was granted. Discovery is not suspended while the pleadings are being finalized. Supplemental responses to be provided by January 29, 2021. Sanctions denied since Defendants' opposition was filed before Judge Quidachay ruled on the first demurrer.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Defendant Angela Oliveri, First Western Properties, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Angela Oliveri, First Western Properties, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Demurrer to causes of action for indemnity is moot; dismissal on file. Demurrer is otherwise sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing the existence of a written contract or establishing exception to the statue of frauds. Plaintiff shall also allege facts in support of each element of breach of fiduciary duty. The Court will not follow Afont at this time, but may do so when the cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is properly asserted if, as such, it appears completely duplicative of the cause of action for professional negligence. Plaintiff shall further allege misrepresentation with particularity (when, how, by what means, where, etc), especially as against a corporate defendant. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Defendant Scott Laeber, Colliers International Ca, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Scott Laeber, Colliers International Ca, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Demurrer to causes of action for indemnity is moot; dismissal on file. Demurrer is otherwise sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to the specific duty to investigate zoning. References made in opposition to general statutes establishing obligations of the brokers and general references to the Letter of Intent/Lease are not sufficient. The issue here is not the existence of a client/broker relationship, the issue is the specific duty to investigate zoning - the source of this duty is not currently alleged. Plaintiff shall further allege misrepresentation with particularity.Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
